methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Agile Robots is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zhaopeng Chen and Peter Meusel, currently valued above $1B having raised approximately $384M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies spanning one public listing and eight private entities, including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median method was applied to the comparable universe. The nine robotics peers ranged from $300M to $10.0B in valuation, with a median of $1.6B. This method directly implies a $1.6B valuation for Agile Robots, anchored by contemporaneous peers such as Hadrian (Series C, $1.6B) and Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B), which provide strong stage and sector alignment.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B to $1.8B, centered at a median of $1.6B. The current stated valuation of >$1B sits comfortably within and above this range. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion observed in peer valuations—particularly the $10.0B outlier (1X Technologies)—and limited public market comparables, which constrains cross-validation. Stage calibration across the peer set also introduces variance, as the range spans Series B through Series D. The single applicable valuation method reflects data constraints rather than fundamental disagreement across methodologies.
